purpose: Modular dark-fantasy ruin kit for the explorable outside-town expedition area

conventions.transforms: rotation and scale applied before export

ruin_stone.style: dark slate, irregular mineral grain, broken fissures, restrained moss
ruin_stone.upgrade_target: 2K physically scaled CC0 photogrammetry material from Poly Haven or ambientCG
ruin_stone.rule: Reuse the selected final scanned material on the ruin kit instead of creating unique generic noise textures per mesh

ruin_granite_scanned.style: cool dark rough-hewn granite with scanned mineral and fracture detail
ruin_granite_scanned.rule: Preserve the 1.9m physical texture span and reuse this material for the production ruin family

ruin_dark_rock.style: charcoal monolithic rock with broad fractures, fine fissures, and no painted masonry joints
ruin_dark_rock.rule: Use for individually modeled monoliths and hero ruins; preserve the 2.4m physical span and let geometry define every masonry joint

quality_targets.final_material_source: scanned or photogrammetry-based PBR with recorded license and physical scale

modules.quality_review: Blockout-approved; requires final scanned material and a more varied stone/fracture pass before environment final
design_language: asymmetric collapsed silhouette
staggered chunky masonry
irregular broken crown
restrained integrated rubble
readable from the gameplay camera
modules.placement_status: Placed in EXPEDITION_RUIN_PLACEMENTS; overlaps Low B and uses seam rubble to conceal modular ends

modules.quality_review: Production irregular-masonry revision complete: 31 individually warped stones, uneven staggered courses, broken seam lines, chipped corners, randomized depth/lean, seven angular rubble chunks, physically scaled 2K CC0 granite PBR, and optimized Draco/WebP delivery
design_language: asymmetric broken saddle silhouette
surviving left shoulder
central gameplay sightline dip
collapsed right end
six uneven courses with no continuous ruler-straight seam
31 stones with unique corner warping, depth offsets, and rotations
seven angular fractured rubble chunks
grid-compatible with ruin-wall-tall-a
modules.placement_status: Placed in EXPEDITION_RUIN_PLACEMENTS as overlapping wall runs with broken ends hidden behind rubble

modules.quality_review: Massive gate-fragment v2 approved against the improved outside-town reference: broad displaced fracture planes, large monolithic stones, thick partially buried supports, a nearly continuous radial arch with one believable crown failure, grounded boulder-scale rubble, sparse moss, homogeneous dark-rock PBR, neutral/night review, and optimized Draco/WebP delivery. Replaces rejected small-brick v1.
design_language: massive ancient gate fragment rather than stacked brick towers
upper-right crown failure with most of the arch curve still readable
tall load-bearing left monolith and shorter right survivor
clear navigable central portal
large radial voussoirs locked into thick spandrel masses
faceted geometric surface displacement and broad chipped planes
six boulder-scale collapsed masses and sparse moss ledges
no texture-painted masonry courses or doubled seam pattern
grid-compatible 4m modular footprint
modules.collision_hint: Use two broad simplified pier colliders, keep the central portal open, and add only low optional rubble boxes
modules.placement_status: Placed off-axis as non-interactive architecture; expedition-gate.glb remains the only functional portal

modules.quality_review: Production monumental column complete: exact modular footprint, broad stepped plinth, single continuous tapered octagonal shaft, flared capital and oversized abacus, controlled faceted displacement, weathered non-load-bearing edges, restrained base rubble, homogeneous dark-rock PBR, neutral/night review, and optimized Draco/WebP delivery
design_language: structurally intact and vertically stable
one continuous tapered shaft rather than stacked wall blocks
broad partially buried stepped plinth
heavy dark-fantasy capital readable at gameplay distance
large fracture planes with controlled edge damage
three restrained base rubble pieces
vegetation supplied by separate environment scatter assets
2m square modular placement footprint
modules.collision_hint: Use one tapered shaft/pedestal collider plus a broad base box; ignore the three small rubble pieces
modules.placement_status: Placed in the west arch group and east foundation group

modules.quality_review: Production broken-column companion complete: exact shared footprint, matching four-level plinth, continuous tapered octagonal stump, directional jagged shear crown, side-readable fallen shaft length, two capital fragments, three subordinate rubble chips, homogeneous dark-rock PBR, neutral/night review, and optimized Draco/WebP delivery
design_language: same architectural family and placement grid as ruin-column-intact
upper mass sheared toward the front-right
highest jagged shaft rim survives toward the back-left
recognizable horizontal octagonal shaft fragment
two fallen capital fragments concentrated with the collapse
limited subordinate debris rather than a uniform rubble halo
vegetation supplied by separate environment scatter assets
2m square modular placement footprint
modules.collision_hint: Use a broad base box plus a short tapered stump collider; ignore all loose fragments
modules.placement_status: Placed as asymmetric companions to the intact columns

modules.quality_review: Production large landmark rubble complete: 70/25/5 hierarchy, three dominant interlocked masses, recognizable fallen column/capital/arch fragments, four medium bridge pieces, minimal perimeter chips, broad faceted displacement, all dominant undersides sunk into the terrain plane, homogeneous dark-rock PBR, neutral/night review, and optimized Draco/WebP delivery
design_language: three dominant masses establish one readable silhouette
recognizable fallen octagonal column length, capital slab, and arch voussoir
four medium fragments bridge rather than scatter the main forms
only four subordinate perimeter chips
dominant masses penetrate the ground plane instead of floating
1.55m maximum height preserves useful gameplay sightlines
vegetation and crystals supplied by separate environment scatter assets
4m by 3m modular placement footprint
modules.collision_hint: Use three simplified dominant-mass boxes plus an optional fallen-column capsule; ignore medium and small fragments
modules.placement_status: Placed as paired mid-ground path-framing masses with distinct rotations and scale

modules.quality_review: Production small rubble complete: compact 70/25/5 hierarchy, two interlocked dominant masses, recognizable fallen octagonal shaft fragment, three medium bridge fragments, three restrained perimeter chips, exact grounded pivot, homogeneous dark-rock PBR, neutral/night review, and optimized Draco/WebP delivery
design_language: one strong asymmetric fallen-slab silhouette
secondary fracture mass interlocks rather than forming a separate pile
recognizable octagonal shaft fragment reads from the gameplay camera
three medium bridge fragments connect the dominant forms
only three subordinate perimeter chips
0.8m maximum height keeps the prop easy to see and walk around
vegetation and crystals supplied by separate environment scatter assets
2m by 1.6m modular placement footprint
modules.collision_hint: Use one low dominant-mass box; ignore the shaft and loose fragments
modules.placement_status: Placed at wall overlaps and column transitions to conceal seams and ground architecture

modules.quality_review: Production foundation slab complete: buried continuous footing, broad stable center, six fitted cap plates with non-repeating seams and height variation, exposed perimeter construction, one localized collapsed corner, homogeneous dark-rock PBR, neutral/night review, and optimized Draco/WebP delivery
design_language: broad load-bearing center rather than a decorative tiled floor
large fitted plates create architectural scale
irregular perimeter and chipped planes prevent a perfect modern rectangle
localized front-right collapse concentrates damage instead of scattering it uniformly
approximately 2.3m by 2.1m stable central support zone
low profile transitions cleanly between terrain and vertical ruins
vegetation and debris supplied by separate environment scatter assets
4m square modular placement footprint
modules.collision_hint: Use one 4m square low box collider and omit the localized corner rubble
modules.placement_status: Placed partially buried beneath the west arch and east column group

modules.quality_review: Production distant watchtower complete: broad four-spine shell, true lancet opening, asymmetric left-heavy surviving crown, localized upper-right failure, grounded base collapse, physically scaled dark-rock PBR, and optimized Draco/1K WebP delivery
design_language: broad watchtower silhouette readable through expedition fog
four unequal octagonal corner spines
real front and rear negative-space openings
left-heavy crown survival with a localized upper-right collapse
few large architectural masses instead of foreground brick noise
restrained grounded rubble concentrated below the failed crown
modules.collision_hint: Skyline placement should remain outside the playable ring; omit gameplay collision unless reused as an approachable landmark
modules.placement_status: Placed six times across mid/far depth bands in EXPEDITION_SKYLINE_PLACEMENTS and instanced as one draw-call batch

modules.quality_review: Production split-belfry silhouette complete: narrower forked massing, tall open belfry, rear stair-turret counterform, front-right directional collapse, fallen cornice, physically scaled dark-rock PBR, and optimized Draco/1K WebP delivery
design_language: narrower split belfry distinct from Tower A
forked surviving crown around a true open span
rear octagonal stair turret creates a secondary silhouette
front-right collapse direction reinforced by a fallen cornice
large distant-readable fracture planes
minimal rubble and no uniform damage halo
modules.collision_hint: Skyline placement should remain outside the playable ring; omit gameplay collision unless reused as an approachable landmark
modules.placement_status: Placed six times across mid/far depth bands in EXPEDITION_SKYLINE_PLACEMENTS and instanced as one draw-call batch

modules.quality_review: Grounded compression-arch v2 replaces the rejected floating silhouette: nine mutually bearing voussoirs form a complete load path into oversized imposts, overlapping monolithic piers, independent buried plinths, and deep rear buttresses. Damage is localized to the upper-right outer spandrel and four grounded rubble masses without interrupting the structural arch. The model retains homogeneous dark-rock PBR, no portal treatment, and optimized Draco/1K WebP delivery.
design_language: wide horizontal skyline landmark balancing the two vertical towers
large open architectural span with no magical portal language
complete nine-stone compression ring visibly seated into both impost blocks
broad overlapping piers and separate buried plinths instead of disconnected block stacks or a thin continuous shelf
localized upper-right spandrel erosion preserves the arch load path
deep buttresses and side remnants preserve a believable three-dimensional civic ruin
four restrained grounded rubble masses concentrated below the failed outer wall
modules.collision_hint: Skyline placement should remain outside the playable ring; if brought forward, use two pier boxes and keep the central opening clear
modules.placement_status: Placed six times across mid/far depth bands in EXPEDITION_SKYLINE_PLACEMENTS and instanced as one draw-call batch

next_model_checklist: Use ruin_wall_low_b revision production-irregular-masonry-v3 as the current quality and scale reference
Model at real meter scale beside ruin_wall_low_b and preserve the declared modular footprint
Define an asymmetric silhouette, negative spaces, and collapse direction before building individual stones
Apply large-, medium-, and small-scale variation; texture noise alone does not count as geometric variation
Stagger joints and vary stone height, depth, front plane, lean, chipped corners, and fracture planes independently
Reject ruler-straight seams, repeated joint positions, a shared front plane, and staircase crowns made from intact boxes
Use angular unique rubble shapes rather than repeated cubes or smooth spheres
Use geometry for silhouette and medium damage; use the shared PBR material for microdetail
Choose material by modeling strategy: never project a scan containing masonry courses onto separately modeled stones; use homogeneous ruin_dark_rock at 2.4m for monoliths and hero ruins
Use a high-to-low bake for hero or close-view modules
Review untextured and flat-shaded geometry before judging the material
Review in neutral lighting and the game night lighting
Keep the bottom-center pivot at ground level
Export only the selected module from the active Blender scene
Disable animation, camera, and light export
Add the module and its eventual world transform to this manifest
